MOTION TO INTERVENE
Off Calendar Jan-14-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Kimberly Chu, Esq. of Brayton Purcell LLP, Tel. 415.898.1555, appearing via Zoom for the Plaintiffs;
Todd Thacker, Esq. of Goldberg Segalla LLP, Tel. 415.432.6600, appearing for Intervenors Transcontinental Insurance Company, Industrial Indemnity Company, and Fireman's Fund Insurance Company.

This matter is contested. Argument is held.

At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling with the following modification: the motion is conditionally granted on the production of the applicable policies within twenty days of the order.

Intervenors Transcontinental Company, Industrial Indemnity Company, and Fireman's Fund Insurance Company, Amended Motion to Leave to Intervene on Behalf of Enterprise Plumbing, Inc., is GRANTED under C.C.P. section 387(d)(2). Opposition filed by Plaintiffs. Reply filed.

The Court GRANTS Intervenors' Request for Judicial Notice. The Court exercises its discretion to permit Intervenors to intervene. Intervenors represent they are the insurers for Enterprise Plumbing, Inc., a terminated California corporation that lacks the ability to defend itself. (See Thacker Decl. 5 ["If Enterprise Plumbing, Inc. were an active corporation, Intervenors would provide a defense under a reservation of rights under their respective insurance policies."].) Since Intervenors would be liable for any ensuing default judgment, they have a direct interest in this litigation and their interests will be prejudiced unless they are allowed to intervene. (See Ins. Code 11580(b)(2); Western Heritage Ins. Co. v. Superior Court (2011) 199 Cal.App.4th 1196, 1204-1205, citing Reliance Ins. Co. v. Superior Court (2000) 84 Cal.App.4th 383, 385-388 [insurer may intervene where it is liable for default judgment]; Kaufman & Broad Communities, Inc. v. Performance Plastering, Inc. (2006) 136 Cal.App.4th 212, 216 [insurer must intervene to protect its rights where its insured is a suspended corporation defendant].) Intervenors' admittedly sparse showing is sufficient; at this stage, Intervenors need not identify or attach a copy of the applicable policy or policies, which Plaintiffs may obtain through discovery, nor show that they do not contain asbestos exclusions. Thus, the Court finds good cause to allow the intervention.

Mr. Thacker is to prepare and submit a proposed order that tracks the tentative ruling with the modification as stated in open court.
